schools.summary: Gill: Deborah is named first, being the root of the work, chief both in directing the war and composing the song.

themes: Then sang Deborah and Barak the son of Abinoam on that day
the deliverance answered with a song of praise to the LORD
I will sing unto the LORD, for he hath triumphed gloriously (Exod. 15:1)

schools.summary: Gill: the avenging of the injuries done to Israel by their enemies, particularly Jabin for twenty years; the people willingly offered themselves.

themes: Praise ye the LORD for the avenging of Israel, when the people willingly offered themselves
God praised both for his salvation and for the willing hearts he gives
thy people shall be willing in the day of thy power (Ps. 110:3)

schools.summary: Gill: she desires that all potentates, far and near, might hear of the wonderful works of God for his people; she would sing unto the LORD God of Israel.

themes: Hear, O ye kings; give ear, O ye princes; I, even I, will sing unto the LORD
the glory of God published before the highest of the earth
I will speak of thy testimonies also before kings, and will not be ashamed (Ps. 119:46)

schools.summary: Gill: here properly begins the song, with an apostrophe to the Lord, recalling ancient appearances of God for his people.

themes: LORD, when thou wentest out of Seir... the earth trembled, and the heavens dropped, the clouds also dropped water
the LORD coming in majesty to deliver his people
the mountains quake at him... the earth is burned at his presence (Nah. 1:5)

schools.summary: Gill: the inhabitants of the mountains melted through fear, the Lord going before Israel in a pillar of cloud and fire.
Keil & Delitzsch: the mountains melting before the LORD.

themes: The mountains melted from before the LORD, even that Sinai from before the LORD God of Israel
nothing standing before the glory of God
the mountains flowed down at thy presence (Isa. 64:1)

schools.summary: Gill: during the twenty years of Jabin's oppression, robbers made the highways dangerous, so men travelled by crooked paths; the highways were unoccupied.

themes: the highways were unoccupied, and the travellers walked through byways
the desolation that sin and bondage bring upon the land
the ways of Zion do mourn... her gates are desolate (Lam. 1:4)

schools.summary: Gill: the Canaanite robbers entered the villages and unwalled towns, plundering houses, so the villages ceased; Deborah arose a mother in Israel.

themes: The inhabitants of the villages ceased... until that I Deborah arose, that I arose a mother in Israel
God raising up a deliverer, a mother to his people, in the darkest time
as one whom his mother comforteth, so will I comfort you (Isa. 66:13)

schools.summary: Gill: Israel chose new gods, and this refers to their idolatry, after which war came; there was not a shield or spear among forty thousand.

themes: They chose new gods; then was war in the gates: was there a shield or spear seen among forty thousand in Israel?
apostasy bringing war and helplessness on God's people
they provoked him... therefore he gave his people over to the sword (Ps. 78:58-62)

schools.summary: Gill: most Jewish commentators interpret this of the wise men and scribes willing to teach the people the law even in trouble.

themes: My heart is toward the governors of Israel, that offered themselves willingly among the people. Bless ye the LORD
the willing servants of God honoured, their readiness his gift
all things come of thee, and of thine own have we given thee (1 Chr. 29:14)

schools.summary: Gill: in Judea it was honourable to ride on asses, especially white ones, used by persons of distinction; those who sit in judgment and walk by the way are called.

themes: Speak, ye that ride on white asses, ye that sit in judgment, and walk by the way
every rank of God's people called to publish his deliverance
let every thing that hath breath praise the LORD (Ps. 150:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the army delivered from the archers of Sisera at the river Kishon, or maidens delivered at the wells; there they would rehearse the righteous acts of the LORD.

themes: there shall they rehearse the righteous acts of the LORD, even the righteous acts toward the inhabitants of his villages
deliverance turning daily life into a theatre of praise
we have heard with our ears... what work thou didst in their days (Ps. 44:1)

schools.summary: Gill: she arouses herself to attend the service with more ardour, or finding herself more impelled by the Spirit; Barak is called to lead his captivity captive.

themes: Awake, awake, Deborah: awake, awake, utter a song: arise, Barak, and lead thy captivity captive
the soul roused to praise; Barak's triumph foreshadowing Christ's
when he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive (Eph. 4:8)

schools.summary: Gill: the few mean and miserable that remained under Jabin's yoke were raised to a high estate and dominion over the mighty.

themes: Then he made him that remaineth have dominion over the nobles... the LORD made me have dominion over the mighty
God exalting the abased above the mighty
he raiseth up the poor out of the dust... to set them among princes (1 Sam. 2:8)

schools.summary: Gill: Deborah mentions the tribes that sided and assisted, beginning with Ephraim; out of Zebulun came they that handle the pen of the writer.

themes: Out of Ephraim was there a root... after thee, Benjamin... out of Machir came down governors, and out of Zebulun they that handle the pen
God honouring every willing contribution to his cause
having then gifts differing according to the grace... let us wait on our ministering (Rom. 12:6-7)

schools.summary: Gill: the princes of Issachar came to Mount Tabor to offer themselves; for the divisions of Reuben there were great thoughts of heart.

themes: the princes of Issachar were with Deborah... For the divisions of Reuben there were great thoughts of heart
the decisive set against the divided, indecision keeping men from God's cause
a double minded man is unstable in all his ways (James 1:8)

schools.summary: Gill: this tribe abounded with flocks, and at this critical juncture judged it wisest to attend to their own concerns.

themes: Why abodest thou among the sheepfolds, to hear the bleatings of the flocks?
the love of ease keeping God's people from his service
no man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life (2 Tim. 2:4)

schools.summary: Gill: Gilead abode beyond Jordan; Dan remained in ships and Asher on the sea shore, distinguished from those who came.

themes: Gilead abode beyond Jordan: and why did Dan remain in ships? Asher continued on the sea shore
God's people putting private interest before his cause
look not every man on his own things, but... on the things of others (Phil. 2:4)

schools.summary: Gill: these two tribes were chiefly concerned in the war, the 10,000 that followed Barak, who willingly offered themselves and jeoparded their lives.

themes: Zebulun and Naphtali were a people that jeoparded their lives unto the death in the high places of the field
the noblest service to hazard all for God's cause
they loved not their lives unto the death (Rev. 12:11)

schools.summary: Gill: the kings that came with Sisera, fighting near the same place where Joshua had fought.
Keil & Delitzsch: the kings of Canaan in Taanach by the waters of Megiddo.

themes: The kings came and fought, then fought the kings of Canaan in Taanach... they took no gain of money
God frustrating the greedy expectations of the wicked
the expectation of the wicked shall perish (Prov. 10:28)

schools.summary: Gill: either the angels of heaven, or the heavens and elements, fought against Sisera, a violent storm of rain and hail discomfiting his army.

themes: They fought from heaven; the stars in their courses fought against Sisera
the LORD commanding all creation to fight for his people
the LORD thundered with a great thunder... and discomfited them (1 Sam. 7:10)

schools.summary: Gill: Sisera's army was drawn to Kishon, and many in their confusion were swept away and drowned; Deborah says, O my soul, thou hast trodden down strength.

themes: The river of Kishon swept them away, that ancient river, the river Kishon. O my soul, thou hast trodden down strength
God using the waters to sweep away the proud foe
the LORD on high is mightier than the noise of many waters (Ps. 93:4)

schools.summary: Gill: the hoofs were broken through the force of the waters, or through the swift haste they made to run away.

themes: Then were the horsehoofs broken by the means of the pransings, the pransings of their mighty ones
the strength of the wicked failing in the day of judgment
an horse is a vain thing for safety: neither shall he deliver any by his great strength (Ps. 33:17)

schools.summary: Gill: Deborah said this under a spirit of prophecy, from the Spirit of God, or suggested by an angel; they came not to the help of the LORD against the mighty.

themes: Curse ye Meroz... because they came not to the help of the LORD, to the help of the LORD against the mighty
no neutral ground in God's cause; the failure to help condemned
he that is not with me is against me (Matt. 12:30)

schools.summary: Gill: Jael is blessed under the same prophetic influence that Meroz was cursed, the one for not helping Israel, the other for doing it.

themes: Blessed above women shall Jael the wife of Heber the Kenite be, blessed shall she be above women in the tent
God owning those who serve his cause where others stand aloof
blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness (Matt. 5:6)

schools.summary: Gill: Sisera asked it of her when he turned into her tent.
Keil & Delitzsch: her bringing butter in a lordly dish.

themes: He asked water, and she gave him milk; she brought forth butter in a lordly dish
the providence of God using even courtesy to bring his purpose to pass
when they shall say, Peace and safety; then sudden destruction cometh (1 Thess. 5:3)

schools.summary: Gill: she took up a pin from her tent, with which it was fastened to the ground, and clapped it to the temples of Sisera.

themes: with the hammer she smote Sisera... when she had pierced and stricken through his temples
God bringing down the mighty by the meanest means
God hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the... mighty (1 Cor. 1:27)

schools.summary: Gill: at her first stroke she had done the business so effectually that he dropped at once.
Keil & Delitzsch: the threefold falling, marking his death.

themes: At her feet he bowed, he fell, he lay down... where he bowed, there he fell down dead
the LORD laying the mightiest oppressor utterly in the dust
they are brought down and fallen: but we are risen, and stand upright (Ps. 20:8)

schools.summary: Gill: she looked toward the high road, expecting Sisera to return in his chariot with his victorious army; she cried, Why is his chariot so long in coming?

themes: The mother of Sisera looked out at a window... Why is his chariot so long in coming?
the emptiness of the wicked's expectations, mocked by the event
the triumphing of the wicked is short (Job 20:5)

schools.summary: Gill: her ladies, or maids of honour, each in turn endeavoured to comfort her and make her easy; she returned answer to herself.

themes: Her wise ladies answered her, yea, she returned answer to herself
the self-deceiving of the anxious heart with false comfort
Peace, peace; when there is no peace (Jer. 6:14)

schools.summary: Gill: she supposed they had found the enemy and the spoil, and were dividing it.
Keil & Delitzsch: the imagined prey, a damsel or two to every man.

themes: have they not divided the prey; to every man a damsel or two; to Sisera a prey of divers colours
the vain dreams of the proud and covetous, mocked by reality
thou fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee (Luke 12:20)

schools.summary: Gill: not only a wish but a prophecy: the enemies perish, the lovers of God shine.
Ellicott: the closing prayer and the rest of the land.

themes: let all thine enemies perish, O LORD: but let them that love him be as the sun when he goeth forth in his might. And the land had rest forty years
the certain end of God's enemies and the shining destiny of his friends
then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father (Matt. 13:43)
